title: Drymaria engleriana
taxonomy: Drymaria engleriana is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. This family of plants is commonly known as the pink or carnation family. As a member of the Caryophyllaceae family, Drymaria engleriana is further classified in the genus Drymaria, which is a group of plants that are typically annual or perennial herbs. The genus Drymaria is placed in the order Caryophyllales, which is a group of flowering plants that are often found in dry or rocky areas. At the kingdom level, Drymaria engleriana is classified as a member of the kingdom Plantae, which includes all types of plants.
uses: Drymaria engleriana is reportedly used in traditional medicine for various purposes. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. In some regions, the plant is used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant is also used to alleviate fever and reduce pain. Additionally, it is thought to have antioxidant properties that can help protect against oxidative stress.
